title: GFL, Secure Win Shareholder OK for Merger in Defeat for Abrams
summary: GFL Environmental Inc. and Secure Waste Infrastructure Corp. received shareholder approval for their planned merger, overcoming opposition from investor Abrams Capital Management, according to people familiar with the vote.

summary: Source: Bloomberg, 6:19

title: Knicks Blowout Prompts NYC Bar to Slash Tabs by 37%: ‘We Took One for the Team’
summary: When the owners of one New York City bar bet big on the Knicks, they didn’t expect to be cutting prices by a whopping 37%.

title: Index Funds Can’t Say No to SpaceX
summary: Also fake carbon credits, Survivor bets, Fernando Tatis Jr. update, list-erasing technology and book recommendations.

title: USAA’s CFO Doesn’t Want the Company to Make Too Much Money
summary: The insurer’s mutual-ownership structure shapes a different philosophy toward corporate finance, prioritizing military families.

title: US & Iran Closing in on Interim Deal, Court Says No to AL Map
summary: Source: Bloomberg, 5:58

title: Asian Stocks Hit Record on Chip Rally, Oil Drops: Markets Wrap
summary: Asian shares climbed to a record, driven by renewed momentum in technology shares and easing geopolitical tensions. Oil slipped, while the dollar weakened.

title: Memory Chip Frenzy Sends SK Hynix, Micron Into $1 Trillion Club
summary: The breakneck surge in memory-chip stocks is intensifying, sending the market capitalizations of SK Hynix Inc. and Micron Technology Inc. above $1 trillion for the first time, as investors bet the AI boom will lead to a sustained revaluation of the industry.

title: Oil Dips as US Sees Progress in Iran Deal Despite Fresh Tensions
summary: Oil edged lower on optimism that the US and Iran will reach a peace deal despite fresh hostilities and uncertainty over the vital Strait of Hormuz.

title: UBS’s Khan Says AI Will Have ‘Ramifications’ on Jobs
summary: UBS Group AG’s Asia Pacific President Iqbal Khan said artificial intelligence will free up capacity and improve productivity but also have an impact on jobs.

title: Insurance Mogul Lindberg Gets 12 Years for $2 Billion Fraud
summary: North Carolina investment firm founder Greg Lindberg was sentenced Tuesday to 12 years in prison for siphoning more than $2 billion in reserves backing insurance policies and using the proceeds to pay for jets, mansions and a 214-foot yacht.

title: Iqbal Khan on Geopolitics, AI, and Growth
summary: UBS Asia Pacific Co-President of Global Wealth Management Iqbal Khan speaks to Bloomberg's Stephen Engle as the UBS Asian Investment Conference kicks off in Hong Kong. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: US Touts Iran Deal Prospects Amid Fresh Hormuz Tensions
summary: The US is touting progress toward a peace deal with Iran to end the nearly three-month war, despite fresh hostilities and uncertainty over the Strait of Hormuz. Bloomberg's Derek Wallbank has the latest. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: SQM Boosts Lithium Guidance as Earnings Top Estimates
summary: SQM reported a sharp increase in first-quarter profit and raised its lithium sales forecast, betting that robust demand from battery storage systems will keep the market tightly supplied.

title: Adnoc Exports Another LNG Shipment Through Hormuz to India
summary: Another tanker carrying liquefied natural gas from Abu Dhabi National Oil Co. has exited the Strait of Hormuz, adding to a recent uptick in energy flows through the vital waterway.

title: Gold Holds Loss as Traders Weigh Prospects for US-Iran Deal
summary: Gold held a loss, as fresh clashes in the Persian Gulf weighed on hopes for a US-Iran peace deal and reinforced concerns that inflation could keep interest rates higher for longer.

title: SEC Chairman Eyes ‘Gun-Jumping’ Rule Changes to Spur More IPOs
summary: The Securities and Exchange Commission is mulling changes to decades-old rules that prohibit companies from certain communications during the going-public process, as the Wall Street regulator looks to boost initial public offerings.

title: Trump Backs CFTC as Prediction Market Regulator Amid State Fight
summary: President Donald Trump backed the Commodity Futures Trading Commission as the sole regulator of prediction markets, siding with the agency amid a growing legal fight with states over the multibillion-dollar industry.

title: SpaceX IPO Gets Another Greenlight Toward Faster Index Inclusion
summary: FTSE Russell adopted a rule change that will speed the addition of newly listed large-cap companies to its main indexes, weeks ahead of SpaceX’s expected record-breaking initial public offering.

title: First Brands Hit With US Claim for Alleged Tariffs Fraud
summary: The US government has joined the line of creditors impacted by alleged fraud at bankrupt auto-parts maker First Brands, in this instance for accusations the company cheated on how much it should have paid on tariffs.

title: Enhanced Games Parent Sinks After Troubled Debut Event
summary: Enhanced Group Inc. shares fell to an all-time low after its inaugural sports-entertainment event was marred by technical problems and questions over its only world-record performance, disappointing investors weeks after its public listing.

title: Short Seller Left Tells Jury No Law Barred Post-Research Trades
summary: Short seller Andrew Left told jurors at his criminal securities fraud trial that he doesn’t believe there is anything wrong with him profiting from the “price correction” of a stock after he issues a report or tweet about a company he thinks is overvalued or undervalued.

title: Kardigan Files for IPO to Fund Cardiovascular Disease Treatment
summary: Kardigan Inc. filed for a US initial public offering that will help fund three late-stage drugs that target the root cause of cardiovascular diseases.

title: Activist Ancora Pushes H.B. Fuller to Drop Bid for UK’s AMS
summary: Activist investor Ancora Holdings Group is pushing US adhesives maker H.B. Fuller Co. to abandon a proposed takeover of UK-based Advanced Medical Solutions Group Plc.

title: Rocket, Satellite Stocks Surge as SpaceX IPO Fuels Euphoria
summary: Space and satellite-related stocks soared Tuesday as investor enthusiasm around the industry intensified.

title: Emerging Currencies Slide as Iran War ‘Confusion’ Jolts Markets
summary: Most emerging-market currencies weakened on Tuesday as overnight clashes near the Strait of Hormuz offset signs of progress toward a US-Iran peace deal, even as developing-nation stocks rose on optimism around artificial intelligence.

title: US Steel Costs Impacted by War, Trade Deals
summary: Sam McKinney, KeyBanc Capital Markets vice president and equity research analyst, said that while US steel mills have mostly hedged rising electricity costs with long term contracts, they are impacted by increasing diesel prices to ship steel by truck and rail route. On the other side of the coin, steel hasn't seen a drop in value in in the face of the war, as investors look to steel stocks to hedge against inflation.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: US to Provide Plutonium From Atomic Bombs to Fuel Nuclear Plants
summary: The US is planning to distribute plutonium left over from Cold War-era weapons to commercial nuclear developers as part of the Trump administration’s efforts to accelerate the rollout of new reactors.

title: Optimism on Iran War Deal, AI Theme Push S&P 500 to Record Close
summary: Equities notched their fourth-straight session of gains on Tuesday, with investors hopeful that an agreement to re-open the Strait of Hormuz may be near even as hostilities tested a ceasefire between the US and Iran.

title: MSG Sports Shares Rally to Record as Knicks Reach NBA Finals
summary: Madison Square Garden Sports Corp. hit a fresh record on Tuesday, lifted by investor enthusiasm after the New York Knicks clinched their first NBA Finals berth in nearly three decades.

title: S&P 500 Hits Record High as Iran Peace Hopes Hold: Markets Wrap
summary: Wall Street kicked off the holiday-shortened week with stocks joining bonds higher as hopes for a peace deal between the US and Iran overshadowed military strikes in the Persian Gulf.

title: Jefferies Says Investors Boost ‘Nuclear Exposure’: ESG Investing
summary: Almost two-thirds of fund managers permit some level of “nuclear exposure,” with 34% allowing investments in nuclear weaponry, according to Jefferies Financial Group Inc.’s fourth-annual ESG and defense survey.

title: US Bonds Gain in Catch-Up Trade as Trump Sees Iran Progress
summary: Treasuries rose Tuesday as oil prices remained near their lowest levels of the past month after US President Donald Trump said peace negotiations with Iran were proceeding, even as tensions in the Middle East remain high.

title: Gold Declines as Gulf Strikes Cloud Interest Rate Outlook
summary: Gold fell as military strikes in the Persian Gulf dimmed hopes for a US-Iran peace deal and reinforced concerns that persistent inflation could keep interest rates higher for longer.

title: US Natural Gas Drops in Thin Trading as Options Contracts Expire
summary: US natural gas futures fell just before options contracts for front-month delivery expired, erasing earlier gains that had been triggered by an increase in domestic gas supplies flowing to liquefied natural gas export terminals on the Gulf Coast. Losses were likely aided by a cool shift in weather forecasts, signaling lower demand for gas-fired power to run air-conditioning.
